grambank_features.name: What is the order of numeral and noun in the NP?
grambank_features.gloss: Order of numeral and noun in the noun phrase: 1=Numeral-Noun, 2=Noun-Numeral, 3=both orders occur.

grambank_features.name: What is the pragmatically unmarked order of S and V in intransitive clauses?
grambank_features.gloss: Unmarked order of S and V in intransitive clauses: 1=SV, 2=VS, 3=both.

grambank_features.name: Is the order of constituents the same in main and subordinate clauses?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether constituent order is the same in main and subordinate clauses.

grambank_features.name: Is there clause chaining?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether the language has clause chaining (long strings of dependent clauses + one final finite verb).

grambank_features.name: Is there a morphologically marked distinction between simultaneous and sequential clauses?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether there is a morphological simultaneous-vs-sequential clause distinction.

grambank_features.name: Do collective ('all') and distributive ('every') universal quantifiers differ in their forms or their syntactic positions?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether collective 'all' and distributive 'every' quantifiers differ in form or position.

grambank_features.name: Can polar interrogation be marked by intonation only?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether yes/no questions can be marked by intonation alone.

grambank_features.name: Can polar interrogation be indicated by a special word order?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether yes/no questions can be marked by a special word order.

grambank_features.name: Is there a comparative construction that includes a form that elsewhere means 'surpass, exceed'?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether the comparative uses a form that elsewhere means 'surpass/exceed'.

grambank_features.name: Is there a comparative construction that employs a marker of the standard which elsewhere has a locational meaning?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether the comparative marks the standard with a form that elsewhere is locational ('from/at').

grambank_features.name: Can comparatives be expressed using two conjoined clauses?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether comparatives can be expressed by two conjoined clauses ('X is big, Y is small').

grambank_features.name: Is there a comparative construction with a standard marker that elsewhere has neither a locational meaning nor a 'surpass/exceed' meaning?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether the comparative standard marker has neither a locational nor a 'surpass' source.

grambank_features.name: Is there a bound comparative degree marker on the property word in a comparative construction?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether there is a bound comparative degree marker on the property word.

grambank_features.name: Is there a non-bound comparative degree marker modifying the property word in a comparative construction?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether there is a free comparative degree marker (like 'more') modifying the property word.

grambank_features.name: Can polar interrogation be marked by tone?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether yes/no questions can be marked by tone.

grambank_features.name: Can polar interrogation be indicated by a V-not-V construction?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether yes/no questions can be marked by a V-not-V construction.

grambank_features.name: Do (nominal) content interrogatives normally or frequently occur in situ?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether content interrogatives normally stay in situ (not fronted).

grambank_features.name: Are there three or more distance contrasts in demonstratives?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether demonstratives make three or more distance contrasts (e.g. near/mid/far).

grambank_features.name: Do demonstratives show an elevation distinction?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether demonstratives encode an elevation distinction (up-here vs down-there).

grambank_features.name: Do demonstratives show a visible-nonvisible distinction?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether demonstratives encode a visible-vs-nonvisible distinction.

grambank_features.name: Are there definite or specific articles?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether the language has a definite or specific article (a grammatical word like English 'the').

grambank_features.name: Do indefinite nominals commonly have indefinite articles?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether indefinite nominals commonly take an indefinite article (like English 'a/an').

grambank_features.name: Are there prenominal articles?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether an article, if present, comes before the noun (prenominal).

grambank_features.name: Are there postnominal articles?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether an article, if present, comes after the noun (postnominal).

grambank_features.name: What is the order of adnominal demonstrative and noun?
grambank_features.gloss: Order of an adnominal demonstrative ('this/that') and noun: 1=Dem-Noun, 2=Noun-Dem, 3=both.

grambank_features.name: Can adnominal property words occur discontinuously?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether adjective-like property words can be separated from their noun (occur discontinuously).

grambank_features.name: Is there a gender distinction in independent 3rd person pronouns?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether independent 3rd-person pronouns mark a gender distinction (he vs she).

grambank_features.name: Is there a dual or unit augmented form (in addition to plural or augmented) for all person categories in the pronoun system?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether every person category has a dual (or unit-augmented) form in addition to plural.

grambank_features.name: Are there demonstrative classifiers?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether the language has demonstrative classifiers.

grambank_features.name: Is there productive morphological dual marking on nouns?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether there is productive morphological dual marking on nouns.

grambank_features.name: Is there productive morphological plural marking on nouns?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether there is productive morphological plural marking on nouns.

grambank_features.name: Is there an associative plural marker for nouns?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether there is an associative plural marker ('X and those associated with X').

grambank_features.name: Is there a productive morphological pattern for deriving an action/state noun from a verb?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether an action/state noun can be productively derived from a verb (e.g. 'run' -> 'running').

grambank_features.name: Is there a productive morphological pattern for deriving an agent noun from a verb?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether an agent noun can be productively derived from a verb (e.g. 'teach' -> 'teacher').

grambank_features.name: Is there a productive morphological pattern for deriving an object noun from a verb?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether an object/result noun can be productively derived from a verb.

grambank_features.name: Is there a gender/noun class system where sex is a factor in class assignment?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether the gender/noun-class system uses biological sex as a class-assignment factor.

grambank_features.name: Is there a gender/noun class system where shape is a factor in class assignment?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether the gender/noun-class system uses shape as a class-assignment factor.

grambank_features.name: Is there a gender/noun class system where animacy is a factor in class assignment?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether the gender/noun-class system uses animacy as a class-assignment factor.

grambank_features.name: Is there a gender/noun class system where plant status is a factor in class assignment?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether the gender/noun-class system uses plant status as a class-assignment factor.

grambank_features.name: Are there numeral classifiers?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether the language has numeral classifiers (a sortal element required when counting).

grambank_features.name: Are there possessive classifiers?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether the language has possessive classifiers.

grambank_features.name: Is the adnominal possessive construction different for alienable and inalienable nouns?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether the possessive construction differs for alienable vs inalienable nouns (e.g. 'my dog' vs 'my hand').

grambank_features.name: What is the pragmatically unmarked order of adnominal possessor noun and possessed noun?
grambank_features.gloss: Unmarked order of possessor noun and possessed noun: 1=Possessor-Possessed, 2=Possessed-Possessor, 3=both.

grambank_features.name: Are verbs reduplicated?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether verbs undergo reduplication.

grambank_features.name: Are nouns reduplicated?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether nouns undergo reduplication.

grambank_features.name: Are elements apart from verbs or nouns reduplicated?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether words other than verbs or nouns undergo reduplication.

grambank_features.name: Is there productive morphological trial marking on nouns?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether there is productive morphological trial marking on nouns.

grambank_features.name: Is there productive morphological paucal marking on nouns?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether there is productive morphological paucal marking on nouns ('a few').

grambank_features.name: Can an adnominal property word agree with the noun in gender/noun class?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether an adjective-like property word can agree with its noun in gender/noun class.

grambank_features.name: Can an adnominal demonstrative agree with the noun in gender/noun class?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether an adnominal demonstrative can agree with its noun in gender/noun class.

grambank_features.name: Can an article agree with the noun in gender/noun class?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether an article can agree with its noun in gender/noun class.

grambank_features.name: Can the verb carry a marker of animacy of argument, unrelated to any gender/noun class of the argument visible in the NP domain?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether the verb marks the animacy of an argument (independent of NP-level gender/class).

grambank_features.name: Can an adnominal property word agree with the noun in number?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether an adjective-like property word can agree with its noun in number.

grambank_features.name: Can an adnominal demonstrative agree with the noun in number?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether an adnominal demonstrative can agree with its noun in number.

grambank_features.name: Can an article agree with the noun in number?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether an article can agree with its noun in number.

grambank_features.name: Is there a gender system where a noun's phonological properties are a factor in class assignment?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether a noun's phonological shape is a factor in gender/class assignment.

grambank_features.name: What is the order of adnominal property word and noun?
grambank_features.gloss: Order of adjective-like property word and noun: 0=not used attributively, 1=Adj-N, 2=N-Adj, 3=both.

grambank_features.name: Can an adnominal numeral agree with the noun in gender/noun class?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether an adnominal numeral can agree with its noun in gender/noun class.

grambank_features.name: What is the order of the adnominal collective universal quantifier ('all') and the noun?
grambank_features.gloss: Order of the collective 'all' quantifier and noun: 0=no such quantifier, 1=Q-N, 2=N-Q, 3=both.

grambank_features.name: Can predicative possession be expressed with a transitive 'habeo' verb?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether predicative possession can use a transitive 'have' (habeo) verb.

grambank_features.name: Can predicative possession be expressed with an S-like possessum and a locative-coded possessor?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether predicative possession can use a locative-coded possessor ('at me is a dog').

grambank_features.name: Can predicative possession be expressed with an S-like possessum and a dative-coded possessor?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether predicative possession can use a dative-coded possessor ('to me is a dog').

grambank_features.name: Can predicative possession be expressed with an S-like possessum and a possessor that is coded like an adnominal possessor?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether predicative possession can use a possessor coded like an adnominal possessor.

grambank_features.name: Can predicative possession be expressed with an S-like possessor and a possessum that is coded like a comitative argument?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether predicative possession can use a comitative-coded possessum ('I am with a dog').

grambank_features.name: Is there a clause-initial polar interrogative particle?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether there is a clause-initial yes/no question particle.

grambank_features.name: Is there a clause-final polar interrogative particle?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether there is a clause-final yes/no question particle.

grambank_features.name: Is there a polar interrogative particle that most commonly occurs neither clause-initially nor clause-finally?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether there is a yes/no question particle sitting neither clause-initially nor clause-finally.

grambank_features.name: Can polar interrogation be marked by a question particle and verbal morphology?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether yes/no questions can be marked by a question particle plus verbal morphology.

grambank_features.name: Can standard negation be marked by a non-inflecting word ("auxiliary particle")?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether standard negation can be marked by a non-inflecting negative particle.

grambank_features.name: Is there a phonologically free passive marker ("particle" or "auxiliary")?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether there is a free (particle/auxiliary) passive marker.

grambank_features.name: Is there a phonologically free antipassive marker ("particle" or "auxiliary")?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether there is a free (particle/auxiliary) antipassive marker.

grambank_features.name: Are there special adnominal possessive pronouns that are not formed by an otherwise regular process?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether there are special adnominal possessive pronouns not formed by a regular process.

grambank_features.name: Is dual number regularly marked in the noun phrase by a dedicated phonologically free element?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether dual number is marked in the NP by a dedicated free (unbound) element.

grambank_features.name: Is plural number regularly marked in the noun phrase by a dedicated phonologically free element?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether plural number is marked in the NP by a dedicated free (unbound) element.

grambank_features.name: Is trial number regularly marked in the noun phrase by a dedicated phonologically free element?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether trial number is marked in the NP by a dedicated free (unbound) element.

grambank_features.name: Is paucal number regularly marked in the noun phrase by a dedicated phonologically free element?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether paucal number is marked in the NP by a dedicated free (unbound) element.

grambank_features.name: Is there a large class of nouns whose gender/noun class is not phonologically or semantically predictable?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether there is a large class of nouns whose gender/class is not phonologically or semantically predictable.

grambank_features.name: Can adnominal possession be marked by a prefix on the possessor?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether adnominal possession can be marked by a prefix on the possessor.

grambank_features.name: Can adnominal possession be marked by a prefix on the possessed noun?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether adnominal possession can be marked by a prefix on the possessed noun.

grambank_features.name: Can adnominal possession be marked by a suffix on the possessor?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether adnominal possession can be marked by a suffix on the possessor.

grambank_features.name: Can adnominal possession be marked by a suffix on the possessed noun?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether adnominal possession can be marked by a suffix on the possessed noun.

grambank_features.name: Can mood be marked by a non-inflecting word ("auxiliary particle")?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether mood can be marked by a non-inflecting particle.

grambank_features.name: Can aspect be marked by a non-inflecting word ("auxiliary particle")?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether aspect can be marked by a non-inflecting particle.

grambank_features.name: Can tense be marked by a non-inflecting word ("auxiliary particle")?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether tense can be marked by a non-inflecting particle.

grambank_features.name: Is there a decimal numeral system?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether the numeral system is decimal (base 10).

grambank_features.name: Is there synchronic evidence for any element of a quinary numeral system?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether there is any synchronic evidence of a quinary (base 5) element.

grambank_features.name: Is there synchronic evidence for any element of a vigesimal numeral system?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether there is any synchronic evidence of a vigesimal (base 20) element.

grambank_features.name: Is there a body-part tallying system?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether there is a body-part tallying counting system.

grambank_features.name: Are nominal conjunction and comitative expressed by different elements?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether noun-conjoining ('X and Y') and comitative ('X with Y') use different markers.

grambank_features.name: Is there nonphonological allomorphy of noun number markers?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether noun number markers show nonphonological (lexically/grammatically conditioned) allomorphy.

grambank_features.name: Are there several nouns (more than three) which are suppletive for number?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether more than three nouns are suppletive for number (a different root for singular vs plural).

grambank_features.name: Is there productive overt morphological singular marking on nouns?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether there is productive overt singular marking on nouns.

grambank_features.name: Are there morphological cases for oblique non-pronominal NPs (i.e. not S/A/P)?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether oblique (non-core) non-pronominal NPs carry morphological case.

grambank_features.name: Are there prepositions?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether the language has prepositions.

grambank_features.name: Are there postpositions?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether the language has postpositions.

grambank_features.name: Are variations in marking strategies of core participants based on TAM distinctions?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether core-argument marking varies by TAM (tense/aspect/mood) — e.g. split by tense.

grambank_features.name: Are variations in marking strategies of core participants based on person distinctions?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether core-argument marking varies by person (e.g. a person-based split in ergativity).

grambank_features.name: Can the recipient in a ditransitive construction be marked like the monotransitive patient?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether the recipient of a ditransitive ('give') can be marked like a monotransitive object.

grambank_features.name: Do clausal objects usually occur in the same position as nominal objects?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether clausal objects sit in the same position as nominal objects.

grambank_features.name: Is there a morpho-syntactic distinction between predicates expressing controlled versus uncontrolled events or states?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether there is a morphosyntactic distinction between controlled and uncontrolled events/states.

grambank_features.name: Is there a phonologically or morphosyntactically definable class of ideophones that includes ideophones depicting imagery beyond sound?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether there is a definable class of ideophones depicting imagery beyond sound.

grambank_features.name: Is there an inclusory construction?

grambank_features.name: Is singular number regularly marked in the noun phrase by a dedicated phonologically free element?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether singular number is marked in the NP by a dedicated free (unbound) element.

grambank_features.name: Is there grammatical marking of direct evidence (perceived with the senses)?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether there is grammatical marking of direct (sensory) evidence.

grambank_features.name: Is there grammatical marking of indirect evidence (hearsay, inference, etc.)?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether there is grammatical marking of indirect evidence (hearsay, inference).

grambank_features.name: Is there any accusative alignment of flagging?

grambank_features.name: Is there any ergative alignment of flagging?

grambank_features.name: Is there any neutral alignment of flagging?

grambank_features.name: Is there a male/female distinction in 2nd person independent pronouns?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether 2nd-person independent pronouns mark a male/female distinction.

grambank_features.name: Is there a male/female distinction in 1st person independent pronouns?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether 1st-person independent pronouns mark a male/female distinction.

grambank_features.name: Is there a phonologically independent reflexive pronoun?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether there is a phonologically independent reflexive pronoun.

grambank_features.name: Is there a phonologically independent non-bipartite reciprocal pronoun?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether there is a phonologically independent (non-bipartite) reciprocal pronoun.

grambank_features.name: Are there morphological cases for non-pronominal core arguments (i.e. S/A/P)?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether non-pronominal core arguments (S/A/P) carry morphological case.

grambank_features.gloss: Whether verbs take suffixes/enclitics beyond pure S/A/P agreement (incl. portmanteau agreement+TAM).

grambank_features.name: Is there productive infixation in verbs?

grambank_features.gloss: Whether the S (intransitive subject) can be indexed by a prefix/proclitic on the verb.

grambank_features.name: Can the A argument be indexed by a suffix/enclitic on the verb in the simple main clause?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether the A (transitive subject) can be indexed by a suffix/enclitic on the verb.

grambank_features.gloss: Whether verb stems change according to the person of a core participant.

grambank_features.gloss: Whether standard negation can be marked by an affix, clitic, or verb modification.

grambank_features.name: Is there directional or locative morphological marking on verbs?

grambank_features.name: Is there verb suppletion for tense or aspect?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether verbs are suppletive for tense or aspect.

grambank_features.name: Can tense be marked by an inflecting word ("auxiliary verb")?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether tense can be marked by an inflecting auxiliary verb.

grambank_features.name: Is verb compounding a regular process?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether verb compounding is a regular process.

grambank_features.name: Are there verb-adjunct (aka light-verb) constructions?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether there are verb-adjunct (light-verb / coverb) constructions.

grambank_features.name: Is incorporation of nouns into verbs a productive intransitivizing process?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether noun incorporation into the verb is a productive intransitivizing process.

grambank_features.name: Is there an existential verb?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether the language has a dedicated existential verb ('there is').

grambank_features.name: Are different posture verbs used obligatorily depending on an inanimate locatum's shape or position (e.g. 'to lie' vs. 'to stand')?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether posture verbs ('lie' vs 'stand' vs 'sit') are obligatorily chosen by an inanimate's shape/position.

grambank_features.name: Is there a notably small number, i.e. about 100 or less, of verb roots in the language?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether the language has a small closed set of verb roots (about 100 or fewer).

grambank_features.name: Is a pragmatically unmarked constituent order verb-initial for transitive clauses?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether the unmarked transitive constituent order is verb-initial.

grambank_features.name: Is a pragmatically unmarked constituent order verb-medial for transitive clauses?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether the unmarked transitive constituent order is verb-medial.

grambank_features.name: Is a pragmatically unmarked constituent order verb-final for transitive clauses?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether the unmarked transitive constituent order is verb-final.

grambank_features.name: Is the order of core argument (i.e. S/A/P) constituents fixed?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether the order of core (S/A/P) constituents is fixed.

grambank_features.name: Can standard negation be marked clause-finally?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether standard negation can be marked clause-finally.

grambank_features.name: Can standard negation be marked clause-initially?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether standard negation can be marked clause-initially.

grambank_features.name: Is there a difference between imperative (prohibitive) and declarative negation constructions?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether prohibitive (negative imperative) negation differs from declarative negation.

grambank_features.name: Is there a morphological passive marked on the lexical verb?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether there is a morphological passive marked on the lexical verb.

grambank_features.name: Is there a morphological antipassive marked on the lexical verb?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether there is a morphological antipassive marked on the lexical verb.

grambank_features.name: Is there a morphologically marked inverse on verbs?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether there is a morphological inverse marker on the verb.

grambank_features.name: Is there an overt verb marker dedicated to signalling coreference or noncoreference between the subject of one clause and an argument of an adjacent clause ("switch reference")?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether there is a switch-reference marker (signals same/different subject across clauses).

grambank_features.name: Are causatives formed by affixes or clitics on verbs?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether causatives are formed by affixes/clitics on the verb.

grambank_features.name: Is there a causative construction involving an element that is unmistakably grammaticalized from a verb for 'to say'?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether there is a causative built from a grammaticalized verb 'to say'.

grambank_features.name: Can polar interrogation be indicated by overt verbal morphology only?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether yes/no questions can be marked by verbal morphology alone.

grambank_features.name: Can standard negation be marked by an inflecting word ("auxiliary verb")?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether standard negation can be marked by an inflecting negative auxiliary verb.

grambank_features.name: Does the verb for 'give' have suppletive verb forms?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether the verb for 'give' has suppletive forms.

grambank_features.name: Can the agent be expressed overtly in a passive clause?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether the agent can be expressed overtly in a passive clause ('by ...').

grambank_features.name: Are there multiple past or multiple future tenses, distinguishing distance from Time of Reference?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether there are multiple past or future tenses distinguishing distance in time (e.g. recent vs remote past).

grambank_features.name: Is there overt morphological marking on the verb dedicated to mood?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether there is dedicated overt mood marking on the verb.

grambank_features.name: Is there an interrogative verb for content interrogatives (who?, what?, etc.)?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether there is a dedicated interrogative verb for content questions ('do-what?').

grambank_features.name: Are all person categories neutralized in some voice, tense, aspect, mood and/or negation?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether all person categories are neutralized in some voice/TAM/negation context.

grambank_features.name: Is there a class of patient-labile verbs?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether there is a class of patient-labile verbs (same verb form used transitively and intransitively with the patient as subject).

grambank_features.name: Does the verb for 'see' have suppletive verb forms?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether the verb for 'see' has suppletive forms.

grambank_features.name: Does the verb for 'come' have suppletive verb forms?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether the verb for 'come' has suppletive forms.

grambank_features.name: Is there a preposed complementizer in complements of verbs of thinking and/or knowing?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether there is a preposed complementizer for complements of 'think/know' verbs.

grambank_features.name: Is there a postposed complementizer in complements of verbs of thinking and/or knowing?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether there is a postposed complementizer for complements of 'think/know' verbs.

grambank_features.name: Can the S or A argument be omitted from a pragmatically unmarked clause when the referent is inferrable from context ("pro-drop" or "null anaphora")?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether the S or A argument can be dropped when inferrable from context (pro-drop / null anaphora).

aus_extension.name: Position of case affixes
aus_extension.gloss: Where case marking sits: suffix, prefix, clitic, tone, stem change.

aus_extension.name: Case marked separately on pronouns vs nouns
aus_extension.gloss: Whether both non-pronominal core arguments and pronominal core arguments carry case (so case can differ by word class).

aus_extension.name: A argument indexed on the verb
aus_extension.gloss: Whether the transitive subject (A) is cross-referenced by a bound marker on the verb.

aus_extension.name: P argument indexed on the verb
aus_extension.gloss: Whether the transitive object (P) is cross-referenced by a bound marker on the verb.

aus_extension.name: Nominal dual number
aus_extension.gloss: Productive morphological dual on nouns.

aus_extension.name: Nominal paucal number
aus_extension.gloss: Productive morphological paucal ('a few') on nouns.

aus_extension.name: Nominal trial number
aus_extension.gloss: Productive morphological trial (exactly three) on nouns.

aus_extension.name: Coding of nominal plurality
aus_extension.gloss: How nominal plural is coded: suffix, prefix, reduplication, plural word, clitic, none.

aus_extension.name: Distance contrasts in demonstratives
aus_extension.gloss: Number of distance contrasts (2, 3+ deictic terms).

aus_extension.name: Gender in independent 3rd-person pronouns
aus_extension.gloss: Whether independent 3rd-person pronouns mark gender (he/she).

aus_extension.name: Verb conjugation classes
aus_extension.gloss: Whether verbs fall into conjugation classes (as in most Pama-Nyungan languages).

aus_extension.name: Position of tense-aspect affixes
aus_extension.gloss: Where TAM inflection sits on the verb: prefix, suffix, tone, mixed, none.

aus_extension.name: Multiple (distance) past/future tenses
aus_extension.gloss: Whether there are multiple past or future tenses distinguishing temporal distance (e.g. recent vs remote past) — common in Australian languages.

aus_extension.name: Associated motion / verbal directional marking
aus_extension.gloss: Directional or locative morphology on the verb. NOTE: true associated-motion systems (coming/going while V-ing) are a fine Australianist category that open datasets do not isolate; this uses Grambank's broader directional/locative-on-verb feature as a proxy.

aus_extension.name: Verb suppletion for participant number
aus_extension.gloss: Whether a verb uses a different root for singular vs plural participant (e.g. 'go.sg' vs 'go.pl').

aus_extension.name: Reduplication productivity
aus_extension.gloss: Whether reduplication is productive and of what kind (full/partial).

aus_extension.name: Bound reflexive marker on verb
aus_extension.gloss: A phonologically bound reflexive marker on the verb (e.g. Kuku Yalanji -ji-).

aus_extension.name: Bound reciprocal marker on verb
aus_extension.gloss: A phonologically bound reciprocal marker on the verb (e.g. Kuku Yalanji -wa-).

aus_extension.name: Morphological antipassive
aus_extension.gloss: A morphological antipassive on the lexical verb (demotes P; salient in ergative languages).

aus_extension.name: Causative by affix/clitic
aus_extension.gloss: Whether causatives are formed by an affix/clitic on the verb.

aus_extension.name: Prohibitive vs declarative negation
aus_extension.gloss: Whether the prohibitive (negative imperative) uses a different construction from declarative negation.

aus_extension.name: Dominant constituent order
aus_extension.gloss: Dominant order of subject, object and verb (often 'no dominant order' in free-word-order Australian languages).

aus_extension.name: Adposition type
aus_extension.gloss: Prepositions vs postpositions vs no dominant adpositions.

aus_extension.name: Switch-reference
aus_extension.gloss: A verb marker signalling same/different subject across clauses.

aus_extension.name: Clause chaining
aus_extension.gloss: Chains of dependent (medial) clauses closed by one finite verb.

aus_extension.name: Pro-drop (null core arguments)
aus_extension.gloss: Whether S/A can be omitted when inferrable — pervasive in bound-pronoun Australian languages.

aus_extension.name: Numeral base system
aus_extension.gloss: Base of the numeral system: decimal, quinary, vigesimal, body-part tally, or restricted (many Australian languages have very small numeral systems).

aus_extension.name: Verb–adjunct (coverb / light-verb) constructions
aus_extension.gloss: Whether predicates are built from an uninflecting coverb + a small closed class of inflecting light verbs (widespread in non-Pama-Nyungan Australia).

aus_extension.name: Noun incorporation (intransitivizing)
aus_extension.gloss: Whether noun incorporation into the verb is a productive intransitivizing process.
